title: Deprecate fab_append_intent and fab_update_registry surfaces
description: Explicitly remove or mark deprecated the fab_append_intent and fab_update_registry MCP tools from user-facing registration and guidance. Update protected tokens, bootstrap templates, docs, tests, and command expectations to point to Event Ledger automatic typed events plus doctor/sync-meta baseline flow.
test: Tests for protected token lint, server tool registration, bootstrap snapshots/templates, and docs references no longer require fab_append_intent or fab_update_registry as core protocol tools.
acceptance_criteria: Newly generated guidance does not tell agents to call fab_append_intent or fab_update_registry; server registration tests reflect deprecation; docs describe .fabric/events.jsonl and automatic MCP typed events.
hints: Requirement says these two are explicitly deprecated. execution_directives: pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-server test -- index --runInBand; pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-cli test -- lint-protected-tokens i18n --runInBand
prev_context:
[Explore deprecation-cli-templates] fab_append_intent/fab_update_registry are hard-coded as core protocol in server tool registration, protected-token lint, bootstrap templates, docs, and hook warnings. Deprecation should keep/alias old MCP tools only if compatibility is required, remove them from bootstrap required-token policy, rewrite templates toward fab_plan_context+fab_get_rule_sections, sync-meta/doctor baseline acceptance, and typed events.jsonl.
[Task T3] Typed Event Ledger instrumentation is in place for surviving MCP/service interfaces, deprecated registrations retained temporarily.
[Task T4] Doctor/sync-meta baseline events are in place.
